findings.title: Audit input itself contained live secrets (chat-channel exposure)
findings.evidence: Audit-request message pasted GitHub PAT and Cloudflare API token verbatim into the chat channel before this audit began.
findings.remediation: Rotate the four exposed secret families and disallow secret pasting outside of secure secret managers.

findings.title: Anonymous callers can execute admin_grant_coin / admin_refund_stake / admin_set_suspension
findings.evidence: POST https://jsykbnkbrwwsxcdurzcw.supabase.co/rest/v1/rpc/admin_set_suspension with the publishable anon key returned HTTP 200 and executed the function. admin_grant_coin returned 23503 (FK error from inside wallet INSERT) — function fully executed. admin_refund_stake returned P0001 stake_not_found — function fully executed. See ADMIN_AUDIT_EVIDENCE.md sections 8-10.
findings.remediation: REVOKE ALL ON FUNCTION public.admin_grant_coin(uuid,numeric,text,text) FROM PUBLIC, anon, authenticated; same for admin_refund_stake and admin_set_suspension. Add the REVOKE block to admin_sprint4.sql so future re-applies do not regress. Add corresponding tests.

findings.title: Live production secrets committed to git and exposed in chat
findings.evidence: Commit 6429c1c added docs/admin/SHASHKI_ROYALE_ADMIN_HANDOFF.docx, which contains the owner password (plaintext), GitHub PAT, Cloudflare API token, Cloudflare Account ID, Supabase anon key, and instructions embedding the PAT in the git remote URL.
findings.remediation: Rotate all four secret families (GitHub PAT, Cloudflare API token, Supabase service role + anon, admin password, JWT_SECRET). Remove the DOCX from main. Schedule a coordinated history rewrite (git filter-repo). Audit any .git/config or shell-history for embedded PAT URLs. Add gitleaks to CI.

findings.title: Idempotency check is non-atomic; wallet mutation and audit insert can split-brain
findings.evidence: Lines 262-311 (grant), 326-375 (refund), 395-450 (suspend) perform: SELECT admin_audit_log -> POST RPC -> INSERT admin_audit_log. If the audit INSERT fails, retries re-execute the RPC. Parallel retries can both pass the SELECT and both mutate.
findings.remediation: Introduce admin_operations table with PRIMARY KEY(idempotency_key); have each RPC accept the key, INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, and return the cached result on conflict. Move audit log emission to a trigger on admin_operations.

findings.title: Migration drops every CHECK constraint matching the substrings 'type' or 'amount'
findings.evidence: Lines 20-31 and 44-55 use pg_get_constraintdef(oid) ~* 'type' and ~* 'amount' as the WHERE clause for DROP CONSTRAINT, removing unrelated invariants on wallet_transactions and any future constraint mentioning those words.
findings.remediation: Write a repair migration that targets constraints by name (or by a stricter regex anchored on column expressions) and reinstates a precise wallet_transactions_amount_check that allows admin_adjustment to be negative without removing positivity for legitimate types.

findings.title: Production source maps exposed
findings.evidence: GET https://shashki-royale-admin.pages.dev/assets/index-8GqLdxc4.js.map returns HTTP 200.
findings.remediation: Set build.sourcemap=false in vite.config.ts and add a _headers rule denying /*.map (defence in depth). Confirm post-deploy that .map paths return 404.

findings.title: JWT stored in localStorage without HttpOnly, combined with no CSP
findings.evidence: Lines 58-59 set sr_admin_token in localStorage. No CSP in production headers (FIND-014). Any XSS yields full admin takeover for 8 hours.
findings.remediation: Issue session as HttpOnly Secure SameSite=Strict cookie from the CF Function. Add CSP. Add server-side revocation.

findings.title: Login timing oracle distinguishes wrong-email from wrong-password
findings.evidence: Wrong-password latency averages ~320 ms, wrong-email ~600 ms (the 250 ms setTimeout fires only on wrong-email branch, line 182-184).
findings.remediation: Always run verifyPassword regardless of email match; gate the final boolean once.

findings.title: No brute-force protection on /api/auth/login
findings.evidence: Five consecutive wrong logins from the same IP produced five 401s with no throttling and no 429. The admin_rate_violations table is never touched by the current router.
findings.remediation: Add a Cloudflare Rate Limiting Rule (10/min/IP). Persist exponential backoff per actor in DB. Optionally add Turnstile.

findings.title: /api/health leaks supabase URL, admin_email, and env-existence flags
findings.evidence: GET /api/health returns supabase url, admin_email and has_* booleans.
findings.remediation: Replace body with {ok:true,version:GIT_SHA}.

findings.title: UNIQUE index on (actor_id, idempotency_key) is ineffective because actor_id is never set
findings.evidence: supabase/admin.sql defines `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ... ON admin_audit_log (actor_id, idempotency_key) WHERE idempotency_key IS NOT NULL`. functions/api/[[path]].ts inserts only actor_email; actor_id is NULL on every row. With NULLs being non-equal, the index does not enforce uniqueness.
findings.remediation: Either drop actor_id from the index (UNIQUE(idempotency_key)) or populate actor_id from the admin_users row (FIND-026). Better: move idempotency into admin_operations table with PK on idempotency_key.

findings.title: Missing CSP, HSTS, COOP/COEP/CORP on admin Pages
findings.evidence: Live HEAD / showed no Strict-Transport-Security, no Content-Security-Policy, no Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy, no Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy.
findings.remediation: Add a strict default CSP, HSTS preload, COOP same-origin, CORP same-origin, and tighten Permissions-Policy. Example block in ADMIN_SECURITY_AUDIT.md.

findings.title: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * served on admin root and API
findings.evidence: Live HEAD / and OPTIONS /api/* both return ACAO: *.
findings.remediation: Set ACAO to https://shashki-royale-admin.pages.dev plus Vary: Origin. Drop * altogether on admin HTML.

findings.title: Realtime publication contents not audited; possible leak of wallet_transactions / profiles to anon
findings.evidence: src/lib/realtime.ts and LiveFeed.tsx subscribe via anon. Without DB access we cannot list pg_publication_tables. If wallet_transactions or profiles is in supabase_realtime with no row filter, anonymous clients can stream them.
findings.remediation: Confirm publication contents; restrict to views that match RLS for anon. Document allowed channels.

findings.title: CSV export likely vulnerable to spreadsheet formula injection via nickname
findings.evidence: Code path exports player nicknames to CSV; nicknames are user-controlled at game-side sign-up. No sanitisation routine observed.
findings.remediation: Prefix any cell starting with =+-@ \t \r with a single quote before emitting.

findings.title: Path parameters propagated into PostgREST filter strings without UUID validation
findings.evidence: Lines 211-217 and similar interpolate ${pid} from route.match directly into /rest/v1/.../?...eq.${pid} queries.
findings.remediation: Validate ${pid}/${sid} with a UUID regex at each route boundary; reject 400 on mismatch.

findings.title: profiles.suspended_until may not be enforced by game backend
findings.evidence: admin_set_suspension only updates the column. No reference to suspended_until found in the admin repository. The audit had no access to the game backend.
findings.remediation: Add matchmaking / stake-creation checks server-side; consider a DB trigger that refuses inserts for suspended players. Verify in game repo audit.

findings.title: /api/admin/wallets/summary scans all wallets unbounded
findings.evidence: Line 458 SELECTs all wallets without LIMIT; in-memory reduce over the result.
findings.remediation: Expose a SECURITY DEFINER admin_wallets_totals() with a SQL aggregate and REVOKE EXECUTE FROM PUBLIC.

findings.title: limit query param not capped on transactions/recent
findings.evidence: Line 491-493 forwards user-supplied limit to PostgREST without bound.
findings.remediation: Math.min(Math.max(parseInt(limit||'50',10)||50, 1), 500).

findings.title: transactions/by-type aggregates in JS over 10k rows; no audit row
findings.remediation: Move aggregation to admin_tx_by_type() SQL function; add audit insert.

findings.title: No CI; manual wrangler deploys with commit-dirty=true
findings.evidence: No .github/workflows present. Handoff documents `npx wrangler pages deploy dist --commit-dirty=true`.
findings.remediation: Introduce GitHub Actions: typecheck, lint, build, smoke + cloudflare/pages-action for production. Remove --commit-dirty.

findings.title: main likely lacks branch protection / required reviewers
findings.evidence: No CODEOWNERS, no workflow, single-actor commit history under bot identity. PAT in handoff implies direct pushes.
findings.remediation: Require PR review and status checks. Confirm via GitHub API after PAT rotation.

findings.title: admin_users table is decorative; identity sourced only from env vars
findings.evidence: admin.sql declares full RBAC schema (role enum, is_active, totp_secret), but functions/api/[[path]].ts never reads or writes the table.
findings.remediation: Move authentication to read from admin_users; honor is_active; update last_login_at/last_login_ip; set audit_log.actor_id correctly.

findings.title: PBKDF2 iterations and salt parsed from stored hash without minimum enforcement
findings.evidence: Lines 71-89 derive iter and salt from the env-supplied hash. An attacker with CF token can plant a weak hash.
findings.remediation: Enforce iter >= 100000 and salt length >= 16.

findings.title: UI claims 120k PBKDF2 iterations; handoff claims 100k cap
findings.evidence: LoginScreen.tsx:77 text vs handoff section 8.3.
findings.remediation: Align documentation/UI to actual env hash; consider showing nothing.

findings.title: Production deploys with --commit-dirty=true; no reproducible build
findings.evidence: Handoff section 1.2 deploy command.
findings.remediation: Remove --commit-dirty; tie deploys to a clean commit SHA via CI.

findings.title: JWT lacks iss/aud claims and kid
findings.evidence: Lines 47-52 sign only sub/role/iat/exp. Verifier only checks signature and exp.
findings.remediation: Add iss, aud, kid; verify iss/aud server-side; index secrets by kid.

findings.title: No JWT revocation; password rotation does not invalidate active sessions
findings.evidence: jwtVerify checks signature + exp only. No store of revoked jti, no min-iat per actor.
findings.remediation: Add admin_sessions table or KV; check jti/min_iat; invalidate on password change and is_active=false.

findings.title: Role is hard-coded 'owner'; verifier does not enforce role per endpoint
findings.evidence: Line 194 mints role='owner'; getAuth returns { email } only.
findings.remediation: Introduce per-endpoint role gate when multi-admin lands. Until then, document the single-owner model.

findings.title: No gitleaks / trufflehog / GitHub secret scanning gate
findings.evidence: Handoff DOCX containing live secrets was committed and pushed without alert.
findings.remediation: Add gitleaks workflow on PRs and pushes; configure GitHub's built-in secret scanning push protection.

findings.title: Legacy VITE_ADMIN_PASSPHRASE still embedded in bundle
findings.evidence: gate.ts references import.meta.env.VITE_ADMIN_PASSPHRASE. Handoff section 1.3 confirms the variable is still set in CF Pages env. Vite embeds any VITE_* into the bundle.
findings.remediation: Delete the env var on CF Pages; remove src/lib/gate.ts and src/components/Gate.tsx; rebuild.

findings.title: Sprint 4 migration grants EXECUTE without revoking PUBLIC
findings.evidence: Lines 113, 179, 221 issue GRANT EXECUTE ... TO service_role with no REVOKE EXECUTE ... FROM PUBLIC.
findings.remediation: Patch the migration to include REVOKE block; re-apply.

findings.title: Refund silently zero-floors locked_balance
findings.evidence: Lines 146-148, 159-161 use GREATEST(locked_balance - v_each, 0).
findings.remediation: RAISE EXCEPTION when locked_balance < v_each; surface 'locked_balance_inconsistent'.

findings.title: Refund mints crypto_balance without verifying a corresponding stake_lock
findings.evidence: admin_refund_stake adds v_each to crypto_balance unconditionally for each player.
findings.remediation: Require a preceding stake_lock wallet_transaction for the stake; otherwise raise inconsistency.

findings.title: Internal Coin column named crypto_balance creates legal/UX confusion
findings.evidence: Coin is described as internal currency without monetary value; column suggests cryptocurrency.
findings.remediation: Plan a coordinated rename to coin_balance with code/SQL/API updates.

findings.title: Audit log coverage gaps on /auth/me, players/:id/audit, transactions/by-type
findings.evidence: Those handlers do not call audit().
findings.remediation: Add view_* audit insertions for every sensitive endpoint.

findings.title: actor_ip captured only on login; all other audit rows have NULL actor_ip
findings.evidence: audit() helper signature does not accept Request; only the /auth/login path passes actor_ip explicitly.
findings.remediation: Pass req into audit(); read cf-connecting-ip; record on every insert.

findings.title: 500 error path leaks raw error text
findings.evidence: Line 534 returns String(err) including PostgREST messages.
findings.remediation: Log internally; respond with {error:'internal', request_id: cf-ray}.

findings.title: apiFetch returns error bodies as data on non-401 responses
findings.evidence: Lines 66-77 only branch on 401; r.json() is returned for any other status.
findings.remediation: Throw on !r.ok with body.error or `http_${r.status}`.

findings.title: No typed handling of network failure
findings.evidence: fetch rejects with TypeError on network failure; not caught.
findings.remediation: try/catch and rethrow Error('network_error').

findings.title: JWT expiration noticed only on next location change
findings.evidence: useEffect at lines 19-22 re-checks session on loc changes only.
findings.remediation: Schedule a timer to setAuthed(false) when expiry hits.

findings.title: New idempotency_key generated per retry — defeats server-side idempotency
findings.evidence: Lines 209/219/231 call crypto.randomUUID() per call; clients can't replay safely.
findings.remediation: Hoist key to component state on modal open; reuse across retries.

findings.title: Double-submit possible inside React batched state
findings.evidence: disabled flag is React state; batched render may not reflect before second click within ms.
findings.remediation: Use useRef-based hard guard.

findings.title: React Query cache not cleared on logout
findings.evidence: clearSession() removes only localStorage keys; QueryClient retains data up to staleTime.
findings.remediation: Call queryClient.clear() inside clearSession().

findings.title: No automated dependency vulnerability scanning
findings.evidence: No Dependabot config, no `yarn npm audit` in CI.
findings.remediation: Add Dependabot + a CI step that runs `yarn npm audit --severity high`.

findings.title: No Subresource Integrity inventory or strategy
findings.evidence: All scripts are first-party (good); no SRI strategy documented for any future CDN reference.
findings.remediation: Add an internal policy: any external script must include `integrity` and `crossorigin` attributes.

findings.title: Real-money Litecoin on-ramp/off-ramp roadmap carries regulatory/AML/gambling exposure
findings.evidence: Handoff section 6 / Sprint 7 details NOWPayments/CoinPayments integration with 1 LTC = 1000 Coin exchange. Combined with the current state (no MFA, no rate limiting, anonymous economy mutations, audit-log split-brain), this multiplies real-world exposure.
findings.remediation: Defer indefinitely. If pursued: counsel review (gambling licensing per jurisdiction; AML/KYC obligations; payment service regulation); architectural review (segregation of funds, immutable audit, fraud workflow); compliance review (data retention, sanctions screening).
